gaps.description: No unit economics, revenue model analysis, or financial justification. Pricing gap ($5K vs $40-60K target) noted as a risk but not analyzed.
gaps.upgradeHint: Add CAC/LTV estimates, competitive pricing comparison, and revenue model for the enterprise push.

gaps.description: Vision is generic marketing copy: 'leading product analytics platform' could describe any competitor. Fails the decision test — doesn't help you say no to anything.
gaps.upgradeHint: Rewrite vision around the specific customer problem (data sprawl at Series B-C SaaS) and the unique capability (pipeline + analytics in one plane).

gaps.description: Pricing acknowledged as too low but no analysis. No willingness-to-pay data, no competitive pricing comparison, no packaging strategy.
gaps.upgradeHint: Add pricing analysis: current ACV distribution, target ACV justification, competitive pricing benchmarks, and packaging tiers.

gaps.description: No buyer-facing value proposition. Differentiation bullets are feature-focused, not connected to buyer outcomes or economic impact.
gaps.upgradeHint: Build a one-page sales enablement brief connecting product capabilities to buyer ROI.

gaps.description: No customer journey mentioned. No touchpoint analysis or post-sale experience design.
gaps.upgradeHint: Map the customer journey from evaluation to expansion. Identify where you lose customers and where you create advocates.

gaps.description: Zero business justification. No ROI calculation. No revenue impact estimate for fixing onboarding. The business case for this project is completely absent.
gaps.upgradeHint: Add a 3-line business case: cost of current churn, expected revenue from improved retention, payback period for the engineering investment.

gaps.description: No stakeholder map. No evidence of engineering buy-in or design alignment. Three open questions left unresolved with no decision owners.
gaps.upgradeHint: Add a RACI matrix, document engineering capacity confirmation, and assign decision owners to each open question.

gaps.description: Beta planned but no experiment design. No hypothesis to test. No success/failure criteria for the beta phase.
gaps.upgradeHint: Frame the beta as a hypothesis: 'We believe auto-detection will increase completion from 34% to 50%. If it doesn't, we'll know X is the blocker.'

gaps.description: Jumps straight to solution. No narrative about why this matters to DataPulse's growth trajectory or customer success story.
gaps.upgradeHint: Open with the business stakes: 'We're losing 66% of signups before they see value. That's $X in unrealized ARR per quarter.'

gaps.description: Features shipped are listed but not connected to customer problems. 'We shipped Smart Alerts V1' doesn't explain what customer outcome it improved.
gaps.upgradeHint: Reframe each shipped item as: problem it solved, customer outcome, evidence it worked.

gaps.description: No post-launch validation data for shipped features. 14/24 using alerts, but no data on whether alerts are actionable or driving retention.
gaps.upgradeHint: Add validation metrics: What % of alerts led to action? Did alert users retain better than non-alert users?

gaps.description: CAC/ACV ratio noted ($3K/$5K) but not analyzed. $120K to $350K ARR projection has no model behind it.
gaps.upgradeHint: Show the math: how many enterprise deals at what ACV gets to $350K? What's the required win rate given the 45-day sales cycle?

gaps.description: First $15K ACV deal mentioned as a win but no analysis of what made it close. No repeatable sales motion described.
gaps.upgradeHint: Analyze the $15K deal: What was the buyer's pain point? What ROI did they expect? How can you replicate this?
